rulings.Case Title: WILEY UTTERBACK et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, AUGUST 2, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Aerojet-General Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted. The court reinstates the tentative ruling from July 19, 2011, which stated: Defendant Aerojet-General Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present any admissible evidence that Wiley Utterback was exposed to any asbestos-containing materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ discovery responses did not give defendant any basis to believe that plaintiffs proceeded on any liability theory other than a premises theory. Accordingly, defendant cannot be faulted for confining its motion for summary judgment to a premises theory and not explicitly addressing a ¿direct negligence¿ theory. Defendant¿s objection to the entirety of the declaration of Russell Utterback is sustained. The court finds that plaintiffs¿ response to special interrogatory 10 dated April 7, 2011 was willfully false and thus subject to exclusion per Biles v. Exxon Mobil Corporation (2004) 124 Cal. App. 4th 1315. Since Wiley Utterback and Russell Utterback are brothers, they worked alongside each other throughout their careers and both are represented by the same law firm in pending asbestos exposure lawsuits (and plaintiff¿s law firm also represents Russell Utterback¿s former wife in another pending lawsuit based on asserted take home exposure derivative from Russell Utterback¿s own exposure), assuming the truth of Russell Utterback¿s declaration filed with the opposition papers, the omission of any reference in response to special interrogatory 10 to Russell Utterback is not reconcilable with the statement in the same response that a ¿reasonable and good faith effort¿ was made to obtain responsive information and no information other than what was stated was learned. rulings.Case Title: RICHARD THEODORE LUKA VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, AUGUST 2, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Lawn-Boy, Inc.¿s demurrer to plaintiffs¿ first amended complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first (negligence), second (strict liability), sixth (survival) and seventh (punitive damages) causes of action and off calendar as to the third (enterprise liability) and fourth (false representation) causes of action. Leave to amend is given to provide plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath. Plaintiffs represent that they withdraw the third and fourth causes of action against defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD THEODORE LUKA VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, AUGUST 2, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Lawn-Boy, Inc.¿s motion to strike punitive damages from plaintiffs¿ first amended complaint is off calendar as moot given the tentative ruling on defendant¿s demurrer. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO PROSCECUTE
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, AUGUST 2, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant Exxon Mobil Corp.¿s motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless counsel for defendant informs Mr. Wilson of the tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. = (220/HEK)
